prompt:
Those lips that Love’s own hand did make,
Breathed forth the sound that said ‘I hate’,
To me that languish’d for her sake:
reference_continuation:
But when she saw my woeful state,
Straight in her heart did mercy come,
Chiding that tongue that ever sweet
Was us’d in giving gentle doom;
SFT_continuation: I love you more than any other.  But when she was old enough to see it all in my eyes and hear what others had told of them—and then they were gone with their lives on; so now is not a day better spent by those who have been lost or forgotten but still live! DPO_continuation: And I love thee more than ever.  And now my dear friend! You are so sweet and gentle; you know how to live with your heart in peace—and yet when thou art gone from thy home alone let us sing again of our beloved mother's beauty as we sang before him on his way back into heaven where he was born anew
GRPO_Rank_continuation: I love you.  But when she was in my womb and with thee to thy breast; then thou dost not see it but on a small white stone of mine face! And now is all time gone by?
